haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students describe physical appearance using adjectives and appropriate vocabulary.
Students discuss personality characteristics and compare people.
Students discuss sports activities and their health benefits.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.2.2 — Expressing ability: could and was/were able to
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use could/was able to to discuss past abilities in sports.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.3.1 — Famous people and their achievements
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students read and discuss biographies of famous people.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.3.2 — Past simple and time expressions
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use past simple with time expressions to narrate life events.
Students discuss endangered species and environmental issues.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.4.2 — Relative clauses: who, which, that
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use relative clauses to give additional information about animals.
Students discuss important inventions and their impact on society.
Students use past passive to describe inventions and discoveries.
Students discuss nutrition and give advice on healthy eating habits.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.6.2 — Conditionals: zero and first conditional
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use zero and first conditional to talk about habits and real situations.
Students compare Turkish and international cultural celebrations.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.7.2 — Present perfect: have/has + past participle
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use present perfect to discuss experiences and recent events.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.8.1 — Health emergencies and first aid
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students discuss health emergencies and describe first aid procedures.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.8.2 — Should/must for advice and obligation
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students give advice and express obligation using modal verbs.
Students give and follow directions; describe city locations.
Students compare cities using comparatives and superlatives.
Students discuss pollution, deforestation and climate change.

haftalar.kazanim: E7.10.2 — Second conditional: if + past, would
haftalar.kazanimDetay: Students use second conditional to discuss hypothetical environmental solutions.
